grammarLessons.name: できます — Can / To Be Able To
好き & 嫌い — Like & Dislike
〜ことが好き / 得意 — Likes & Skills

cards.grammarNote:
Professions need no article: **私は医者です** (I am a doctor) — no 'a' or 'an'.
**医者** (isha) = the common everyday word; **医師** (ishi) = the formal/official term.
cards.phrase: 彼は医者です。

cards.grammarNote:
**看護師** (kangoshi) = **看護** (kango, nursing/care) + **師** (shi, master/specialist) → nursing specialist.
**師** (shi) is a suffix for skilled professionals: **医師** (ishi, doctor), **教師** (kyōshi, teacher), **美容師** (biyōshi, hairstylist).
Compare **〜者** (sha) for practitioners and **〜員** (in) for staff members.
cards.phrase: 彼女は看護師です。

cards.grammarNote:
**エンジニア** (enjinia) is a loanword for engineer.
Alternative: **技術者** (gijutsusha) = **技術** (gijutsu, technology/technique) + **者** (sha, person) → technical person.
**〜者** (sha) is the profession suffix for practitioners: **技術者**, **医者** (isha, doctor).
**兄** (ani) = older brother — Japanese has distinct words for older/younger siblings.
cards.phrase: 兄はエンジニアです。

cards.grammarNote:
**プログラマー** (puroguramā) = loanword for programmer.
**〜として** (to shite) = 'as / in the capacity of': **プログラマーとして働く** = work as a programmer.
This marks a role or capacity, very natural in professional contexts: **学生として** (as a student), **友人として** (as a friend).
cards.phrase: 私はプログラマーとして働いています。
cards.phraseRomanized: watashi wa puroguramā to shite hataraite imasu

cards.grammarNote:
**料理人** (ryōrinin) = **料理** (ryōri, cooking/cuisine) + **人** (nin/hito, person) → cooking person.
**〜人** (nin/jin) is a suffix meaning 'person who does ~': **料理人** (cook), **外国人** (foreigner), **日本人** (Japanese person).
**キッチンで働く** — **で** marks where the work (an action) takes place.
cards.englishPhrase: The cook works in the kitchen.
cards.phrase: 料理人はキッチンで働いています。
cards.phraseRomanized: ryōrinin wa kicchin de hataraite imasu

cards.grammarNote:
**ウェイター** (weitā) = loanword for waiter. Female form: **ウェイトレス** (weitoresu).
In many Japanese restaurants, gender-neutral **店員** (ten'in, shop/restaurant staff) is more common.
**持ってきました** = brought — combining **持つ** (motsu, hold/carry) + **くる** (kuru, come): **持ってくる** = to bring (carry and come).
cards.phrase: ウェイターがメニューを持ってきました。
cards.phraseRomanized: weitā ga menyū wo motte kimashita

cards.grammarNote:
**マネージャー** (manēja) = loanword for manager (especially in business or entertainment contexts).
**部長** (buchō) = department manager/section chief — a common corporate title.
**課長** (kachō) = section chief (lower than 部長).
Japanese corporate hierarchy titles are very specific and important in workplace culture.
cards.phrase: 彼女はマネージャーです。

cards.grammarNote:
**学生** (gakusei) = **学** (gaku, study) + **生** (sei, life/student) → studying life-person.
**生徒** (seito) = a student at a school (elementary through high school).
**学生** is typically used for university students; **生徒** for younger school students.
cards.phrase: 私は学生です。

cards.grammarNote:
**建築家** (kenchikuka) = **建築** (kenchiku, architecture/construction) + **家** (ka, specialist/expert) → architecture expert.
**〜家** (ka) is the suffix for skilled practitioners and experts: **音楽家** (ongakuka, musician), **作家** (sakka, writer), **画家** (gaka, painter), **科学者** is different (sha suffix for scientist).
cards.phrase: 彼は建築家です。

cards.grammarNote:
**弁護士** (bengoshi) = **弁護** (benko, defense/advocacy) + **士** (shi, samurai/qualified person) → qualified defender.
**士** (shi) is a suffix for licensed/qualified professionals: **弁護士** (lawyer), **医師** (doctor), **建築士** (architectural engineer).
**姉** (ane) = older sister — distinct from **妹** (imōto, younger sister).
cards.phrase: 姉は弁護士です。

cards.grammarNote:
**野球** (yakyū) = **野** (ya, field/wild) + **球** (kyū, ball) → field ball — the Japanese word coined for baseball.
**〜を見るのが好き** = like watching ~. **の** nominalizes **見る** (to watch) so **が好き** can follow: 野球を見るのが好き = like the act of watching baseball.
cards.phrase: 野球を見るのが好きです。
cards.phraseRomanized: yakyū wo miru no ga suki desu

cards.grammarNote:
**退職者** (taishokusha) = **退職** (taishoku, retirement) + **者** (sha, person).
**退職** = **退** (tai, retreat/leave) + **職** (shoku, employment/position) → leaving one's position.
**退職しています** (taishoku shite imasu) — the **〜ている** form describes the ongoing state of being retired.
cards.phrase: 父は退職しています。

cards.grammarNote:
**読書** (dokusho) = **読** (doku/yomu, read) + **書** (sho/kaku, write/book) → book reading.
Used as a noun for the hobby of reading: **読書が好き** (I like reading).
Compare **本を読む** (hon wo yomu, read a book) — the activity with a specific object — vs **読書** as the hobby concept.
cards.phrase: 読書が好きです。

cards.grammarNote:
**書くこと** (kaku koto) = the act of writing — **こと** nominalizes the verb **書く** (kaku, to write).
**こと** vs **の** as nominalizers: both work in **〜が好き**, but **こと** is slightly more formal/written.
**物語** (monogatari) = story/tale: **物** (mono, thing) + **語** (gatari/katari, telling/narrative).
cards.phrase: 彼女は物語を書くのが好きです。
cards.phraseRomanized: kanojo wa monogatari wo kaku no ga suki desu

cards.grammarNote:
**絵を描く** (e wo kaku) = to draw/paint pictures.
**絵** (e) = picture/painting; **描く** (kaku/egaku) = to draw/paint.
Note: **書く** (kaku) = to write, while **描く** (also kaku, but written differently) = to draw. The kanji differ but pronunciation is the same.
**大好き** (daisuki) = love/really like — **大** intensifies **好き**.
cards.phrase: 彼は絵を描くのが大好きです。
cards.phraseRomanized: kare wa e wo kaku no ga daisuki desu

cards.grammarNote:
**お絵描き** (oekaki) = casual word for drawing/sketching — the **お** prefix gives it a light, friendly tone.
**絵** (e, picture) + **描き** (kaki, drawing) → picture-drawing.
More formal: **絵を描くこと** (e wo kaku koto). **お絵描き** is especially used for casual, recreational drawing.
cards.phrase: お絵描きが好きです。

cards.grammarNote:
**写真撮影** (shashin satsuei) = **写真** (shashin, photograph) + **撮影** (satsuei, filming/shooting).
**写真** = **写** (sha, copy/reflect) + **真** (shin, truth/real) → real reflection.
**趣味** (shumi) = hobby. To say what your hobby is: **〜が趣味です** (~ is my hobby) or **趣味は〜です** (my hobby is ~).
cards.phrase: 写真撮影が趣味です。

cards.grammarNote:
**音楽** (ongaku) = **音** (on/oto, sound) + **楽** (gaku/raku, music/pleasure) → sound-pleasure.
**音楽家** (ongakuka) = musician — the **〜家** (ka) suffix for skilled practitioners.
**聴く** (kiku) = to listen (to music/sounds) — note the specific kanji **聴**, which emphasizes attentive listening. Compare **聞く** (kiku) = to hear/ask (more general).
cards.englishPhrase: I listen to music every day.
cards.phrase: 毎日音楽を聴きます。

cards.grammarNote:
**歌う** (utau) = to sing. **歌うこと** or **歌うの** = the act of singing (nominalizer turns verb into noun).
**歌** (uta) = song/singing — as a noun: **歌が上手** (good at singing).
**大好き** (daisuki) = love. Pattern: **〜のが大好きです** = love doing ~.
cards.phrase: 彼女は歌うのが大好きです。
cards.phraseRomanized: kanojo wa utau no ga daisuki desu

cards.grammarNote:
The verb for playing an instrument depends on the type:
**弾く** (hiku) for strings/keyboard: **ギターを弾く** (play guitar).
**吹く** (fuku) for wind: **フルートを吹く** (play flute).
**叩く** (tataku) for percussion: **ドラムを叩く** (play drums).
cards.phrase: ギターを弾きます。

cards.grammarNote:
**ダンス** (dansu) = loanword from English 'dance'.
**ダンスが大好き** = love dancing — **ダンス** is used directly as a noun subject with **が大好き**.
Alternative: **踊り** (odori) = traditional dance/dancing (Japanese word): **日本舞踊** (nihon buyō, Japanese classical dance).
**踊る** (odoru) = to dance (the verb).
cards.phrase: 私たちはダンスが大好きです。
cards.phraseRomanized: watashitachi wa dansu ga daisuki desu

cards.grammarNote:
**料理** (ryōri) = cooking/cuisine/dish.
**料理する** or **料理をする** = to cook — many hobbies use **〜をする**: **料理をする**, **サッカーをする**.
**料理するのが好き** — **の** nominalizes **料理する** so **が好き** can follow.
cards.phrase: 料理するのが好きです。
cards.phraseRomanized: ryōri suru no ga suki desu

cards.grammarNote:
**ガーデニング** (gādeningu) = loanword from English 'gardening'.
Traditional Japanese garden work: **庭いじり** (niwa ijiri, tending the garden — informal).
**リラックスできます** = can relax — **できます** is the polite potential form of **できる** (can/be able to).
cards.phrase: ガーデニングはリラックスできます。

cards.grammarNote:
**ハイキング** (haikingu) = loanword from English 'hiking'.
**山でハイキングする** — **で** marks the location where the activity (hiking) happens.
**〜するのが好き** — **の** nominalizes the verb phrase: 'like the act of doing ~'.
cards.englishPhrase: I like hiking in the mountains.
cards.phrase: 山でハイキングするのが好きです。
cards.phraseRomanized: yama de haikingu suru no ga suki desu

cards.grammarNote:
**ランニング** (ran'ningu) = loanword from English 'running'.
Also: **走る** (hashiru) = to run (the verb).
**毎朝ランニングする** = run every morning — **毎朝** (maiasa, every morning) is a time adverb placed before the verb.
**します** after a loanword: many sports and activities use noun + **する**: **ランニングする**, **ジョギングする**.
cards.phrase: 毎朝ランニングします。

cards.grammarNote:
**ジョギング** (jogingu) = loanword from English 'jogging'.
**公園でジョギングする** — **で** marks where the jogging (an action) takes place.
Many physical activities follow the pattern: [place] + **で** + [activity] + **します/する**: **公園でジョギングする**, **山でハイキングする**.
cards.phrase: 彼は公園でジョギングします。
cards.phraseRomanized: kare wa kōen de jogingu shimasu

cards.grammarNote:
**サイクリング** (saikuringu) = loanword from English 'cycling'.
**週末に** — **に** marks a specific time period: **週末に** (on weekends), **毎朝に** (every morning).
**自転車** (jitensha) = bicycle — the Japanese word: **自** (self) + **転** (roll) + **車** (vehicle) → self-rolling vehicle.
cards.phrase: 週末にサイクリングします。

cards.grammarNote:
**水泳** (suiei) = **水** (sui/mizu, water) + **泳** (ei/oyogu, swimming) → water swimming.
**泳ぐ** (oyogu) = the verb 'to swim'.
**水泳が好き** — the noun **水泳** (swimming as a sport/hobby) directly takes **が好き**. Compare: **泳ぐのが好き** (like the act of swimming) — either is natural.
cards.phrase: 水泳が好きです。

cards.grammarNote:
**ヨガ** (yoga) = loanword for yoga.
**ヨガをする** = do yoga — **をする** is the standard pattern for many hobby/activity nouns.
Compare: **ヨガをする** (do yoga), **料理をする** (cook), **サッカーをする** (play soccer).
**毎朝** (maiasa) = every morning; **毎日** (mainichi) = every day; **毎週** (maishū) = every week.
cards.phrase: 彼女は毎朝ヨガをします。
cards.phraseRomanized: kanojo wa maiasa yoga wo shimasu

cards.grammarNote:
**運動** (undō) = **運** (un, move/carry) + **動** (dō, move/motion) → physical movement.
**運動する** = to exercise.
**〜のために** (no tame ni) = for the sake of / for the purpose of: **運動のためにジムに行く** = go to the gym for exercise.
cards.englishPhrase: I go to the gym for exercise.
cards.phrase: 運動のためにジムに行きます。
cards.phraseRomanized: undō no tame ni jimu ni ikimasu

cards.grammarNote:
**旅行** (ryokō) = **旅** (tabi/ryo, journey) + **行** (kō, go/travel) → traveling.
**旅行が大好き** — the noun **旅行** directly takes **が大好き** (love it).
**旅** (tabi) alone means a journey or trip and is often more poetic: **旅に出る** (set out on a journey).
cards.phrase: 旅行が大好きです。

cards.grammarNote:
**キャンプ** (kyanpu) = loanword from English 'camp'.
**キャンプに行く** = go camping — **に行く** after an activity noun means 'go to do ~': **買い物に行く** (go shopping), **キャンプに行く** (go camping).
**夏に** — **に** marks the season/time: in summer.
cards.englishPhrase: We go camping in the summer.
cards.phrase: 夏にキャンプに行きます。

cards.grammarNote:
**ゲーム** (gēmu) = game (loanword). **テレビゲーム** (terebi gēmu) = video game (TV game).
Also: **ビデオゲーム** (bideo gēmu).
**テレビゲームをする** — **をする** is the standard pattern for playing games: **チェスをする** (play chess), **ゲームをする** (play a game).
cards.englishPhrase: He plays video games at night.
cards.phrase: 彼は夜にテレビゲームをします。
cards.phraseRomanized: kare wa yoru ni terebi gēmu wo shimasu

cards.grammarNote:
**テレビを見ること** = the act of watching TV — **こと** nominalizes the verb phrase.
**映画を見るのが好き** in the example — **の** nominalizes here instead of **こと**. Both work in conversational Japanese.
**映画** (eiga) = movie: **映** (project/reflect) + **画** (picture) → projected picture.
cards.phrase: 映画を見るのが好きです。
cards.phraseRomanized: eiga wo miru no ga suki desu

cards.grammarNote:
**聴く** (kiku) = to listen (attentively, to music/audio). The kanji **聴** shows the ear **耳** plus attention.
**聞く** (kiku, same pronunciation) = to hear or to ask — broader and less deliberate.
**〜のが好き** pattern: **音楽を聴くのが好き** = like the act of listening to music.
cards.phrase: 彼女は音楽を聴くのが好きです。
cards.phraseRomanized: kanojo wa ongaku wo kiku no ga suki desu

cards.grammarNote:
**工作** (kōsaku) = **工** (kō, craft/work) + **作** (saku, make/create) → making things by hand.
**手芸** (shugei) = needlework/handicrafts (typically sewing, knitting).
**工作をしています** — **〜をしている** = is doing ~, an ongoing action in progress.
cards.phrase: 子どもたちは工作をしています。
cards.phraseRomanized: kodomotachi wa kōsaku wo shite imasu

cards.grammarNote:
**チェス** (chesu) = loanword from English 'chess'.
**チェスをする** — **〜をする** is the standard pattern for playing board games and many hobbies.
**〜をしますか** = do you ~? / would you ~? — a yes/no question about habits or invitations.
cards.phrase: チェスをしますか？

cards.grammarNote:
**ボードゲーム** (bōdo gēmu) = loanword from English 'board game'.
**週末に** + activity: **に** marks the recurring time (on weekends).
**ボードゲームをする** — **〜をする** with game nouns: **チェスをする**, **ボードゲームをする**, **将棋をする** (play shogi).
cards.englishPhrase: We play board games on weekends.
cards.phrase: 週末にボードゲームをします。
cards.phraseRomanized: shūmatsu ni bōdo gēmu wo shimasu

cards.grammarNote:
**働く** (hataraku) = to work (physical or professional labor). Polite: **働きます**.
**病院で働く** — **で** marks where the work takes place: an action at a location.
**仕事をする** (shigoto wo suru) is also common: to do one's job.
**〜ています** describes the ongoing state of being employed: **病院で働いています** = I work at (am employed at) a hospital.
cards.phrase: 病院で働いています。

cards.grammarNote:
**稼ぐ** (kasegu) = to earn money. The natural verb for earning income.
**もらう** (morau) = to receive — used in the example: **給料をもらう** = receive a salary.
**給料** (kyūryō) = salary: **給** (kyū, provide/supply) + **料** (ryō, fee/amount) → provided amount.
cards.phrase: 彼はいい給料をもらっています。
cards.phraseRomanized: kare wa ii kyūryō wo moratte imasu

cards.grammarNote:
**退職する** (taishoku suru) = to retire/resign from a position.
**去年** (kyonen) = last year. Time words for recent past: **昨日** (kinō, yesterday), **先週** (senshū, last week), **先月** (sengetsu, last month), **去年** (kyonen, last year).
**退職しました** is the polite past — the completed action of retiring.
cards.phrase: 彼女は去年退職しました。

cards.grammarNote:
**仕事がある** = have a job — **がある** expresses possession or existence of an inanimate thing.
**仕事** (shigoto) = work/job: **仕** (ji/shi, serve) + **事** (koto, thing/matter) → serving matter.
Compare: **いい仕事がある** (have a good job) vs **仕事をしている** (am working) — the first is about possession, the second about the ongoing activity.
cards.phrase: いい仕事があります。

cards.grammarNote:
**失業** (shitsugyō) = **失** (shitsu, lose) + **業** (gyō, occupation/business) → losing one's occupation.
**失業している** — the **〜ている** form describes the ongoing state of being unemployed.
**現在** (genzai) = currently/at present — a more formal adverb. Casual: **今** (ima, now).
cards.phrase: 彼は現在失業しています。
cards.phraseRomanized: kare wa genzai shitsugyō shite imasu

cards.grammarNote:
**勉強** (benkyō) = **勉** (ben, diligence/effort) + **強** (kyō, strong/force) → forcing oneself to be diligent.
**勉強する** = to study — a **する** verb (noun + する).
**毎日勉強します** — **毎日** (mainichi) precedes the verb as a time adverb.
cards.phrase: 毎日勉強します。

cards.grammarNote:
**学ぶ** (manabu) = to learn/study (often more profound or deliberate than **勉強する**).
**習う** (narau) = to learn from a teacher (by being taught).
**日本語を勉強しています** in the example uses **勉強する** (study) rather than **学ぶ** — both are correct for language learning.
cards.phrase: 日本語を勉強しています。

cards.grammarNote:
**練習** (renshū) = **練** (ren, knead/train) + **習** (shū, learn/practice) → training through repetition.
**練習する** = to practice — used for skills, music, sports, and language.
**毎日練習します** — practice described as a regular habit using the **ます** form.
cards.phrase: 毎日練習します。

cards.grammarNote:
**教える** (oshieru) = to teach/tell/show. Polite: **教えます** (oshiemasu).
**教師** (kyōshi) = teacher (as a profession): **教** (kyō, teach) + **師** (shi, master).
**〜を教える** — the subject being taught takes **を**: **日本語を教える** (teach Japanese).
cards.phrase: 彼女は日本語を教えています。
cards.phraseRomanized: kanojo wa nihongo wo oshiete imasu

cards.grammarNote:
**建てる** (tateru) = to build/construct (buildings). Transitive — you build something.
**建つ** (tatsu) = to be built / to stand (intransitive — a building comes to stand).
**建設する** (kensetsu suru) = to construct (more formal/large-scale).
**新しい** (atarashii) = new — an **い**-adjective modifying **学校** directly.
cards.englishPhrase: They are building a new school.
cards.phrase: 新しい学校を建てています。

cards.grammarNote:
**直す** (naosu) = to fix/repair/correct (transitive — you fix something).
**直る** (naoru) = to be fixed / to get better (intransitive — something fixes itself).
**直せますか** = can you fix ~? — the potential form **直せる** (naoseru) + **ますか**.
**これを** — **これ** (this) + **を** marks what is being fixed.
cards.phrase: これを直せますか？

cards.grammarNote:
**作る** (tsukuru) = to make/create. Very versatile — used for food, objects, music, etc.
**作る** vs **造る** (tsukuru) — same pronunciation, different kanji: **作る** is general; **造る** is for large structures (ships, buildings).
**コーヒーを作る** — for beverages, **淹れる** (ireru, to brew) is actually more natural than **作る**, but both are understandable.
cards.phrase: 毎朝コーヒーを作ります。

cards.grammarNote:
**デザインする** (dezain suru) = to design — a **する** verb using the loanword **デザイン** (design).
**ウェブサイトをデザインする** — **を** marks what is being designed.
**〜しています** — the ongoing **〜ている** form: he is designing websites (as his current work).
cards.phrase: 彼はウェブサイトをデザインしています。
cards.phraseRomanized: kare wa uebusaito wo dezain shite imasu

cards.grammarNote:
**売る** (uru) = to sell (transitive). Opposite: **買う** (kau, to buy).
**売っています** — the **〜ている** form: describes an ongoing occupation (he sells cars as his work).
**売れる** (ureru) = to sell well / be sold (intransitive): **この車はよく売れる** (this car sells well).
cards.phrase: 彼は車を売っています。
cards.phraseRomanized: kare wa kuruma wo utte imasu

cards.grammarNote:
**買う** (kau) = to buy. Polite: **買います** (kaimasu).
**必要があります** = need to / it is necessary — literally 'the necessity exists'.
Pattern: [verb dictionary form] + **必要があります**: **買う必要があります** = need to buy.
cards.englishPhrase: I need to buy a new computer.
cards.phrase: 新しいコンピューターを買う必要があります。
cards.phraseRomanized: atarashii konpyūtā wo kau hitsuyō ga arimasu

cards.grammarNote:
**助ける** (tasukeru) = to help/save/rescue (often in emergencies or from difficulty).
**手伝う** (tetsudau) = to help/assist (with a task) — more everyday assistance.
**お手伝いしましょうか** = shall I help you? — **お手伝い** is the honorific/polite form of **手伝い** (help/assistance). **しましょうか** = shall I do ~?
cards.phrase: お手伝いしましょうか？

cards.grammarNote:
**管理** (kanri) = **管** (kan, pipe/control) + **理** (ri, reason/manage) → to oversee and control.
**管理する** = to manage/administer.
**チームを管理する** — **を** marks what is managed.
cards.phrase: 彼女はチームを管理しています。
cards.phraseRomanized: kanojo wa chīmu wo kanri shite imasu

cards.grammarNote:
**会う** (au) = to meet/encounter. **集まる** (atsumaru) is used in the example — to gather/assemble.
**毎週月曜日に** — **に** marks the recurring specific day: on Mondays every week.
**集まります** = gather — used when a group meets as a collective.
cards.phrase: 毎週月曜日に集まります。

cards.grammarNote:
**始める** (hajimeru) = to start/begin something (transitive).
**始まる** (hajimaru) = to start/begin (intransitive — something begins on its own).
**8時に仕事を始める** — **に** marks the specific time; **を** marks what is being started.
cards.phrase: 8時に仕事を始めます。

cards.grammarNote:
**終わる** (owaru) = to finish/end (intransitive — the work finishes by itself).
**終える** (oeru) = to finish something (transitive — you finish it).
**5時に仕事が終わります** — **が** marks **仕事** (work) as the subject that finishes; the speaker is not finishing it, it finishes on its own.
cards.phrase: 5時に仕事が終わります。

cards.grammarNote:
**好き** (suki) is an adjective, not a verb. Pattern: **Xが好きです** (I like X).
**楽しむ** (tanoshimu) = to enjoy.
For activities: **Xするのが好きです** (I like doing X).
cards.englishPhrase: I like reading. She likes singing.
cards.phrase: 読書が好きです。彼女は歌うのが好きです。
cards.phraseRomanized: dokusho ga suki desu. kanojo wa utau no ga suki desu

cards.grammarNote:
**リラックスする** (rirakkusu suru) = to relax — loanword from English 'relax' + **する**.
Native Japanese equivalent: **くつろぐ** (kutsurogu) = to relax/feel at ease.
**週末はリラックスします** — **は** marks **週末** (weekends) as the topic: 'as for weekends, I relax'.
cards.phrase: 週末はリラックスします。

cards.grammarNote:
**過ごす** (sugosu) = to spend time / pass time.
**時間を使う** (jikan wo tsukau) is used in the example: **時間を使う** = use time (spend time on something).
**読書に** — **に** marks what the time is devoted to: **読書に時間を使う** = use time on reading.
cards.englishPhrase: I spend a lot of time reading.
cards.phrase: 読書にたくさんの時間を使います。
cards.phraseRomanized: dokusho ni takusan no jikan wo tsukaimasu

cards.grammarNote:
**クリエイティブ** (kurietibu) = loanword for 'creative' — a **な**-adjective.
Native equivalent: **創造的な** (sōzōteki na) = creative/imaginative — **創造** (sōzō, creation) + **的** (teki, -ive/-al suffix).
Before **です** in a predicate, no **な** is needed: **クリエイティブです** (she is creative).
cards.phrase: 彼女はとてもクリエイティブです。

cards.grammarNote:
**面白い** (omoshiroi) = interesting/funny/amusing — an **い**-adjective.
**面白** = **面** (men, face/surface) + **白** (shiro, white) — etymologically 'bright face', but now means interesting/enjoyable.
**興味深い** (kyōmi-bukai) = intellectually fascinating/interesting — a more formal synonym.
cards.phrase: 私の仕事は面白いです。
cards.phraseRomanized: watashi no shigoto wa omoshiroi desu

cards.grammarNote:
**ストレスが多い** (sutoresu ga ōi) = literally 'stress is much/many' → stressful.
**多い** (ōi) = many/much — an **い**-adjective used for countable and uncountable quantities.
**ストレスが多い** is more natural than **ストレスフルな** (loanword stressful) in Japanese speech.
cards.phrase: 私の仕事はストレスが多いです。
cards.phraseRomanized: watashi no shigoto wa sutoresu ga ōi desu

cards.grammarNote:
**退屈** (taikutsu) = **退** (tai, retreat/idle) + **屈** (kutsu, bend/submit) → having nothing to do.
**退屈な** = boring (as a **な**-adjective before a noun).
In a predicate: **退屈です** / **退屈でした** (past). Note: in the example, **退屈でした** uses **で** (connective form of **だ**) + **した** (past).
cards.phrase: 会議は退屈でした。

cards.grammarNote:
**給料** (kyūryō) = **給** (kyū, supply/provide) + **料** (ryō, fee/amount) → provided payment.
**いくら** (ikura) = how much (money) — the standard question word for prices and amounts.
**給料はいくらですか** = what is the salary? (literally: as for salary, how much is it?).
cards.phrase: 給料はいくらですか？

cards.grammarNote:
**スケジュール** (sukejūru) = loanword for schedule.
Native Japanese equivalent: **予定** (yotei) = plan/schedule: **予** (yo, beforehand) + **定** (tei, decide) → advance decision.
**柔軟** (jūnan) = flexible — a **な**-adjective: **柔** (jū, soft) + **軟** (nan, soft/flexible).
cards.phrase: 私のスケジュールは柔軟です。
cards.phraseRomanized: watashi no sukejūru wa jūnan desu

cards.grammarNote:
**休暇** (kyūka) = **休** (kyū, rest) + **暇** (ka/hima, free time) → rest time/vacation.
**お盆** (obon) and **年末年始** (nenmatsu nenshi, New Year period) are the major Japanese vacation periods.
**休暇を取る** = take a vacation — **取る** (toru) here means 'to take' in the sense of taking time off.
cards.englishPhrase: I'm going on vacation next week.
cards.phrase: 来週休暇を取ります。

cards.grammarNote:
**休憩** (kyūkei) = **休** (kyū, rest) + **憩** (kei, relax/rest) → a rest break.
**休憩する** = to take a break.
**〜しましょう** = let's do ~: **休憩しましょう** = let's take a break. The **〜ましょう** form invites or proposes an action.
cards.phrase: 休憩しましょう。

cards.grammarNote:
**オフィス** (ofisu) = loanword for office.
**オフィスで働く** — **で** marks where the working (an action) happens.
**会社** (kaisha) = company/firm (the organization); **オフィス** = the physical space.
**〜ています** describes the ongoing state of employment.
cards.phrase: オフィスで働いています。

cards.grammarNote:
**屋外** (okugai) = **屋** (oku/ya, house/roof) + **外** (gai/soto, outside) → outside the building.
Compare **室内** (shitsunai) = indoors/inside a room.
**外** (soto) is the casual word for 'outside': **外で遊ぶ** (play outside) — simpler than **屋外**.
cards.phrase: 彼女は屋外で働いています。
cards.phraseRomanized: kanojo wa okugai de hataraite imasu

cards.grammarNote:
**面接** (mensetsu) = **面** (men, face) + **接** (setsu, meet/contact) → face-to-face meeting.
**就職面接** (shūshoku mensetsu) = job interview: **就職** (shūshoku, getting a job).
**面接があります** = have an interview — **ある** expresses the existence of an event/appointment.
cards.englishPhrase: I have a job interview tomorrow.
cards.phrase: 明日就職面接があります。

cards.grammarNote:
**同僚** (dōryō) = **同** (dō, same) + **僚** (ryō, colleague/official) → same-rank colleague.
**たち** (tachi) added to make plural: **同僚たち** (colleagues).
**親切** (shinsetsu) = kind/friendly — a **な**-adjective: **親切な人** (a kind person), **親切です** (is kind).
cards.phrase: 同僚たちは親切です。

cards.grammarNote:
**お客様** (okyakusama) = **お** (honorific prefix) + **客** (kyaku, guest/customer) + **様** (sama, honorific suffix) → highly honored guest.
**お客さん** (okyakusan) is less formal but still polite.
**満足している** (manzoku shite iru) = is satisfied — **満足** (manzoku, satisfaction) + **〜している**.
cards.phrase: お客様は満足しています。

cards.grammarNote:
**チーム** (chīmu) = loanword from English 'team'.
**素晴らしい** (subarashii) = wonderful/great — an **い**-adjective.
**チームで働く** — **で** marks the team as the means/context of working: 'work within a team'.
cards.englishPhrase: I work in a great team.
cards.phrase: 素晴らしいチームで働いています。

cards.grammarNote:
**プロジェクト** (purojekuto) = loanword from English 'project'.
**もうすぐ** (mōsugu) = soon/almost — a very common temporal adverb.
**終わります** — the intransitive **終わる** (owaru): the project finishes on its own. Compare: **終えます** (transitive, you finish it).
cards.phrase: プロジェクトはもうすぐ終わります。

cards.grammarNote:
**会議** (kaigi) = **会** (kai, meeting/gathering) + **議** (gi, deliberate/discuss) → a gathering to deliberate.
**会議があります** = have a meeting — **ある** expresses the existence of an event.
**10時に** — **に** marks the specific time: at ten o'clock.
cards.englishPhrase: We have a meeting at ten.
cards.phrase: 10時に会議があります。

cards.grammarNote:
**自由** (jiyū) = **自** (ji, self) + **由** (yū, reason/cause) → self-determined → freedom/liberty.
**自由な時間** = free time (using **な** since **自由** is a **な**-adjective).
**自由時間** (as a compound noun) is also used.
**〜に何をしますか** = what do you do during ~? — **に** marks when the activity occurs.
cards.englishPhrase: What do you do in your free time?
cards.phrase: 自由な時間に何をしますか？
cards.phraseRomanized: jiyū na jikan ni nani wo shimasu ka
